title: meum athamanticum subsp. labranum
intro: Meum athamanticum subsp. labranum, also known as wild chamomile or dog fennel, is a plant species within the family Apiaceae, characterized by its fern-like foliage and small white flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Meum athamanticum subsp. labranum include soothing digestive problems, reducing anxiety and stress, and alleviating menstrual cramps, among others. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used in herbal medicine to harness its therapeutic properties.
